body_txt: The most common stereotype of people from the great white north land of Canada is that they are nice, polite , and apologetic to a fault , with American works depicting Canada as a Quirky Neighbor Country . Not these people . Some Canadians brush off the pleasantries, have no time to talk aboot the weather or any other small talk. In fact, they're just as cold as their weather, and don't give deux merdes about politeness, their apologies have a snarky tone to them, more so when interacting with their Yankee neighbors, Heaven forbid if they're mistaken for their Yankee neighbors, and they are The Dreaded in the likes of ice hockey or warfare by their rivals. Double points if they're from Quebec note usually downplayed in French media when French-Canadians show up, as the accent sounds inherently silly rather than crass to metropolitan French speakers (Which goes both ways, given how equally silly French people might sound to French-Canadians, especially when the former attempts to speak English, which is often downright ridiculous! How's '" zat " for being crass, buddy ?) or Alberta, as both tend to be stereotyped as a Lower-Class Lout region similarly to America's Deep South . Of course, being a rude Canadian doesn't necessarily mean evil; they can be honest and can see the bullcrap, with a passive-aggressive or Affably Evil Canadian being a foil to the character. Part of Stereotype Flip , flipping Courteous Canadian in particular. (That said, it can be combined with Courteous Canadian in the form of a friendly and considerate Canadian who just happens to have a bit of a pottymouth; see, e.g., Kimchee Han in Kim's Convenience , as well as pretty much any appearance by Matty Matheson except The Bear .) See also Evil Brit , "Ugly American" Stereotype , The Bogan , Violent Glaswegian , Dublin Skanger , Northern Irish and Nasty , Football Hooligans , London Gangster , The Yardies , Brooklyn Rage , Half-Witted Hillbilly , The Irish Mob , Small-Town Tyrant , The Quincy Punk and Amoral Afrikaner for more nasties in the Anglosphere. For non-anglospherical variants, there's The Idiot from Osaka , the French Jerk , and Asian Rudeness .
